cooked:
Thanks for this writeup! Can you comment on protocols like STIR and WHIR and sum-check protocols in general? It seems like promising research, especially for proof size and verification speed.

I might be hesitant to commit to an opcode for a specific ZK protocol, which is not provably optimal across all dimensions, but if there exists a basic primitive, like batch polynomial evaluation, which is lightweight and independent of any specific protocol, it might be easier to build consensus for it within the Bitcoin community.
topic_title: Proposal: OP_STARK_VERIFY - Native STARK Proof Verification in Bitcoin Script
topic_html_title: Proposal: OP_STARK_VERIFY - Native STARK Proof Verification in Bitcoin Script
raw:
Thanks for this writeup! Can you comment on protocols like [STIR](https://eprint.iacr.org/2024/390) and [WHIR](https://eprint.iacr.org/2024/1586.pdf) and sum-check protocols in general? It seems like promising research, especially for proof size and verification speed.

I might be hesitant to commit to an opcode for a specific ZK protocol, which is not provably optimal across all dimensions, but if there exists a basic primitive, like [batch polynomial evaluation](https://eprint.iacr.org/2026/448), which is lightweight and independent of any specific protocol, it might be easier to build consensus for it within the Bitcoin community.
